subject: Mathematics
title: Coordinate Geometry Basics
description: A 60-second lesson on Coordinate Geometry Basics.

hook.text: Every point on a flat surface can be described by just two numbers: x and y.

buildup.text: The x-axis runs left-right. The y-axis runs up-down. Together they form a grid.

discovery.text: The point (3, 4) means go 3 right and 4 up. Every location has a unique address.

twist.text: With coordinates, you can calculate distances between points using the Pythagorean theorem.

climax.text: GPS, video games, and every digital map rely on this simple coordinate idea.

punchline.text: Two numbers locate anything on a flat surface.

quiz.question: What does the point (3, 4) describe?
options: A position 3 units right and 4 units up from the origin
